response.justification: Strong staff-level candidate with direct experience in AI-native engineering environments and distributed high-throughput systems (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby). Excellent TypeScript/JavaScript expertise and systems thinking breadth. However, the complete absence of Python—listed as a must-have skill—is a notable gap. Recent re:cinq role demonstrates hands-on work with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks, perfectly aligned with the role's industry focus. Would be worth discussing Python capability.
strengths: Direct AI-native engineering experience at re:cinq: AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks
Proven distributed systems expertise at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests/second with microservices architecture
Strong TypeScript/JavaScript and Node.js across multiple staff-level roles with demonstrated ownership and breadth across full stack
concerns: Python is not mentioned on resume despite being listed as a must-have technical skill; primary languages are JavaScript/TypeScript
On-call rotation and incident-response experience not explicitly documented, despite role requiring operational readiness
Recent 7-month caregiving career gap (Oct 2025–present) raises questions about immediate availability

key_factors.factor: Absence of Python from skill set
